summary: Excellent Miller‑remastered soundboard capture of an ’87 show highlighted by a smoking Maggie’s Farm → Cumberland, Terrapin, Bird Song, Other One and a flawless Stella Blue.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard (Miller) master that lets every instrument shine, even if Brent’s keyboards sit a touch forward in the mix. Musically the concert is a showcase of late‑‘80s Dead energy – Jerry’s soaring solos on Looks Like Rain and Terrapin, vibrant renditions of Maggie’s Farm, Cumberland, Bird Song and an emotive Stella Blue make this a standout performance.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers gave 4‑5 stars and praised both the musical performance and recording quality; the show is described as exceptional with an excellent Miller soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller remaster sounds crisp and clean; good sounding overall, though Brent's keyboard can be a bit loud in the mix. No drop‑outs or major noise issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds strong momentum with Bird Song, Maggie’s Farm and Quinn; the second set keeps the energy high with Terrapin, Cumberland, a focused Other One segue from Space, and a heartfelt Stella Blue finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise his guitar tone, soaring solos (Looks Like Rain, Terrapin) and vocal performance (Stella Blue).
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as “everywhere” on Other One and noted for not taking a verse on Maggie’s Farm, but overall positive.
band_member_comments.Bob: Acknowledged in the space/Other One jam, weaving fast spirals with Jerry and Phil.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work highlighted – violin sample on Maggie’s Farm, mellotron on Terrapin, though occasionally a bit loud in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumz described as adventurous ‘87 samples; drummers “throttle down” for high energy jams.
